message:
fix(mcp,frankenterm): redact ignore_reason alongside title/cwd in pane-state emit

Pane-state payloads carry an optional `ignore_reason` string that a
future agent or user-facing hook can fill with arbitrary text
explaining why a pane was skipped (e.g. "excluded by pattern 'ssh
root@host:secret-deployment'"). Until now the redactors ran only
over `title` and `cwd`, so `ignore_reason` could leak a sensitive
substring straight through the wa.state MCP surface and the ft CLI
list output even though the two sibling fields on the same struct
were sanitized.

Fix: extend both redactors to walk `ignore_reason` through the same
redactor helper.

## crates/frankenterm-core/src/mcp_tools.rs

`redact_mcp_pane_state_fields` gains an extra
`if let Some(ignore_reason) = state.ignore_reason.as_mut() { *ignore_reason
= REDACTOR.redact(ignore_reason); }` branch alongside the title and
cwd branches.

Existing test `wa_state_redaction_helper_scrubs_title_and_cwd` is
renamed to
`wa_state_redaction_helper_scrubs_title_cwd_and_ignore_reason` and
its fixture state now also populates
`ignore_reason: Some(format!("exclude-{redaction_fixture}"))`, so the
"raw secret leaked in wa.state JSON" assertion must additionally
scrub the ignore_reason field.

## crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs

`redact_pane_state_fields_for_output` — the sibling helper used by
`ft list` (both JSON and human) — grows the same `ignore_reason`
branch, calling `redact_for_output(...)` which is the CLI-side
counterpart of the MCP redactor.

The existing redaction-shape regression test in the same file
gains `ignore_reason: Some(format!("exclude-{secret}"))` so the
`json.contains(secret) == false` assertion covers the new field.

No public API / struct changes. Both redactors are pure helpers
that take `&mut` of the state they redact, so callers that do not
populate `ignore_reason` (the common case) are unaffected.
